nodes.name: Send Email: S4.1-E1 | Your Numbers Are Almost Final

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "Your numbers are almost final — here's the one step left", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
One visit. Exact numbers. No guesswork left on the table. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

You've already done the hard part.

You've looked at the estimate, thought through what changes once someone actually measures, and seen why the process behind the quote matters as much as the number itself.

At this point, there's one step between an estimate and a number tied to your actual home: the Measurement Verification.

This is not a sales visit.

It's the part where we document the actual openings, assess the existing conditions around them, and confirm the scope against what is really there instead of what a calculator has to assume.

That matters because the estimate is a starting range. Verification is what turns that range into something you can actually use.

Sometimes that means the number changes because the project is simpler than expected. Sometimes it means something hidden gets caught before it becomes your problem on installation day. Either way, you find out before you commit, not after.

If the verified number works for you, great. If it doesn't, you still leave with accurate information about your home and your options.

Schedule Your Measurement Verification

Or reply "verify" and we'll help you find a time that works.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: S4.1-E2 | What Happens During the Verification

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "What actually happens during a Measurement Verification", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
What the visit covers — and why people see the project differently after. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

A lot of homeowners hear "Measurement Verification" and assume it means someone comes out, measures a few openings, and hands over a quote.

It's more useful than that.

What we're really doing is checking the parts of the project an online estimate cannot see.

That includes the actual dimensions of each opening, the condition of the surrounding frame and substrate, and the conditions that affect what the final scope really is once the work is tied to your home instead of a standard assumption.

That is why people usually walk away from the visit seeing the project differently than they did beforehand.

Not because they got a sales pitch.

Because they finally have a clear picture of what is really there.

For some homeowners, that means realizing the job is more straightforward than they expected. For others, it means catching a condition that would have turned into a delay, a change order, or a scope mismatch later.

Either way, the value is clarity.

You leave with a number tied to your actual home, not a model of one.

Schedule Your Measurement Verification

One visit. No obligation. Just real numbers.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}

nodes.name: Send Email: S4.1-E3 | The Part That's Expensive Is Finding Out

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ type:'Email', contactId: $('Get Contact (Entry)').first().json.contact.id, subject: "The part that's expensive is finding out late", html: " Reece Windows & Doors
The problem usually isn't the estimate. It's discovering the real scope after decisions have already been made. ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
Reece Windows & Doors

Hi {{contact.first_name}},

The real risk in a project like this usually isn't getting an estimate.

It's finding out too late that the estimate and the house were never fully aligned.

That is where the expensive part starts.

Not because someone measured carefully.

Because no one did.

When scope questions, opening conditions, or fit issues are discovered after product decisions are already in motion, the homeowner loses the one thing that matters most: control.

Control over the number.

Control over the schedule.

Control over whether to move forward at all.

That is what the Measurement Verification protects.

It gives you the chance to make the next decision with the actual scope in hand instead of learning the truth after choices have already narrowed.

There is nothing wrong with taking more time. There is nothing wrong with getting other quotes.

But it is better to do those things with accurate numbers than with assumptions.

Schedule Your Measurement Verification

And if now isn't the right time, that's fine too. When you're ready to replace the estimate with a verified number, this is the step that gets you there.

Talk soon,

{{custom_values.rep_name}}

Reece Windows & Doors
Protecting families since 1972
{{location.city}}, FL
Unsubscribe  •  © {{right_now.year}} {{location.name}}
", from: "randy@getreecewindows.com", fromName: "Randy Reece" }) }}
